summary: Ukrainian forces destroyed a Russian UAV training centre in Snizhne, killing 65 operators, while Russian strikes continued across multiple fronts, targeting cities such as Krivoy Rog, Kharkiv and Dnipro. UAV activity remains high, with new Russian drone groups entering Sumy and Zaporizhzhia regions and Ukrainian air defenses reporting dozens of shoot‑downs. President Zelensky warned of five Russian scenarios for a northern offensive from Belarus, prompting heightened SBU security measures in the north.
key_points: Ukrainian drones eliminated the Russian UAV operator training centre in Snizhne, Donetsk, killing 65 trainees and the centre chief (Source: @UkraineNow/81356).
Russian forces launched strikes on Krivoy Rog, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Sumy and Kramatorsk, with additional attacks on civilian targets in Belgorod and Bryansk regions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30330).
Russia attempted a drone‑borne attack on Crimea; the Russian defence ministry claimed 167 Ukrainian UAVs were shot down on 20 May (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30330).
New Russian UAV groups entered Sumy region via V. Pysarivka and are heading toward Królevets, increasing pressure on the northern front (Source: @eRadarrua/92824, @kpszsu/62337).
UAV sorties were reported from Poltava toward Myrhorod and Kharkiv, and from Zaporizhzhia toward Kushuhum, indicating expanded drone corridors (Source: @kpszsu/62364, @kpszsu/62350).
Russian tactical aviation activity intensified on the north‑east and south‑east axes, raising the threat of air strikes on frontline areas (Source: @kpszsu/62336, @kpszsu/62360).
A Russian missile used to arm a Geran‑2 drone left elevated radiation on wreckage in Chernihiv region, suggesting possible use of radiological payloads (Source: @UkraineNow/81359).

President Zelensky warned that Russia is preparing a northern offensive from Belarus along the Chernigov‑Kiev axis, prompting SBU security operations in five northern oblasts (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30315).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian forces destroyed a Russian UAV training centre in Snizhne, killing 65 operators, while Russian strikes hit Krivoy Rog, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia and other cities. Drone activity surged with new Russian UAV groups entering Sumy and Zaporizhzhia regions and Ukraine reporting dozens of UAV shoot‑downs. Zelensky warned of five Russian scenarios for a northern offensive from Belarus, leading to heightened SBU security measures in the north.

points: Ukrainian strike drones eliminated the Russian UAV operator training centre in Snizhne, Donetsk, killing 65 trainees and the centre chief "Buryi" (Source: @UkraineNow/81356).
Ukrainian air defence claimed to have shot down 167 Russian UAVs on 20 May, reflecting intense aerial contest over Crimea and the south‑east (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30330).
Ukrainian special‑operations unit "Artem" conducted a successful raid in Stepanivka on the Zaporizhzhia front, seizing enemy supplies (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66856).
Ukrainian forces reported a civilian casualty in Dnipro after a Russian missile strike damaged a five‑storey building, injuring a 58‑year‑old woman (Source: @UkraineNow/81358).
SBU launched extensive security checks in Chernihiv, Kyiv, Zhytomyr, Volyn and Rivne oblasts to prevent infiltration and sabotage (Source: @UkraineNow/81360).
Russian artillery fired at least two strikes on Bohodaryivka and two on Krivoy Rog in Dnipropetrovsk region, indicating continued pressure on industrial hubs (Source: @eRadarrua/92816).
Russian tactical aviation increased activity on north‑east and south‑east axes, threatening frontline districts with air strikes (Source: @kpszsu/62336, @kpszsu/62360).
A Russian missile used to arm a Geran‑2 drone left elevated radiation on wreckage in Chernihiv, suggesting possible radiological payload experimentation (Source: @UkraineNow/81359).
Russian forces reported the downing of 167 Ukrainian UAVs on 20 May, highlighting their anti‑drone capabilities (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30330).
Russian ground forces continued strikes on Krivoy Rog, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Sumy and Kramatorsk, with reported civilian injuries in Belgorod villages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30330).
Ukrainian drones struck Russian positions in the villages of Bely Kolodez, Izbitskoe, Ryasnoe and Staritsa on the Kharkiv front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30330).
Heavy battles persisted near Kupyansk‑Uzlovoy, Kurilovka and Kovsharovka as Ukrainian forces attempted to expand their control zone (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30330).
Ukrainian UAV attacks were reported near Primorsk and Stepnogorsk on the Zaporizhzhia front, increasing pressure on Russian defensive lines (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30330).
Russian GR "Vostok" operated northwest of Alexandrovgrad, engaging Ukrainian forces near the Volchya River and Lesnoe village (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30330).
Ukrainian artillery units in Dnipropetrovsk region directed strike UAVs toward Pavlograd and Shakhtarske, targeting logistics hubs (Source: @eRadarrua/92803).
New Russian UAV groups entered Sumy region via V. Pysarivka, heading toward Królevets, expanding aerial pressure on the northern front (Source: @eRadarrua/92824).
UAVs were observed moving from Poltava toward Myrhorod and from the north of Sumy toward Królevets, indicating coordinated drone corridors (Source: @kpszsu/62364, @kpszsu/62337).
UAVs in central Chernihiv region were sighted heading to Nizhyn, suggesting reconnaissance or strike missions near the frontline (Source: @kpszsu/62341).
President Zelensky warned of a potential Russian offensive from Belarus along the Chernigov‑Kiev axis, prompting SBU security operations in five northern oblasts (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30315).
Russian tactical aviation activity intensified on the north‑east axis, raising the risk of air strikes on frontline settlements (Source: @kpszsu/62336).
Ukrainian forces reported a Russian missile strike in Dnipro, damaging a five‑storey residential building and injuring a civilian (Source: @UkraineNow/81358).
UAV activity from Mykolaiv suburb using a "Banderol" drone was noted, indicating Russian drone deployment in the southern theater (Source: @eRadarrua/92828).
Ukrainian UAVs attacked Russian positions near Krasnaya Liman, with urban battles continuing in the area (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30330).
Russian forces reported increased UAV usage in the south‑east direction, threatening frontline districts (Source: @kpszsu/62360).
UAVs from Zaporizhzhia region moved toward Kushuhum from the south, suggesting pressure on the southern supply routes (Source: @kpszsu/62350).
Ukrainian drones destroyed the Russian UAV training centre in Snizhne, eliminating 65 operators and the centre chief (Source: @UkraineNow/81356).
Russia claimed to have shot down 167 Ukrainian UAVs on 20 May, reflecting intense aerial contest (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30330).
Russian tactical aviation increased activity on north‑east and south‑east axes, raising the threat of air strikes on frontline areas (Source: @kpszsu/62336, @kpszsu/62360).
Ukrainian UAVs conducted strikes on Russian villages in Belgorod and Bryansk regions, injuring civilians and damaging vehicles (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30330).
Radiation detected on wreckage of a Russian missile used to arm a Geran‑2 drone in Chernihiv suggests possible use of unconventional payloads (Source: @UkraineNow/81359).

key_developments: Ukrainian drones eliminated the Russian UAV operator training centre in Snizhne, Donetsk, killing 65 trainees and the centre chief (Source: @UkraineNow/81356).
Russian artillery fired at least two strikes on Bohodaryivka and two on Krivoy Rog in Dnipropetrovsk region, indicating continued pressure on industrial hubs (Source: @eRadarrua/92816).
New Russian UAV groups entered Sumy region via V. Pysarivka, heading toward Królevets, expanding aerial pressure on the northern front (Source: @eRadarrua/92824).
President Zelensky warned of five Russian scenarios for a northern offensive from Belarus along the Chernigov‑Kiev axis, prompting heightened SBU security measures (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30315).
Russian tactical aviation activity intensified on the north‑east and south‑east axes, raising the threat of air strikes on frontline districts (Source: @kpszsu/62336, @kpszsu/62360).
Russia claimed to have shot down 167 Ukrainian UAVs on 20 May, reflecting intense aerial contest (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30330).
Ukrainian forces reported a civilian casualty in Dnipro after a Russian missile strike damaged a five‑storey building (Source: @UkraineNow/81358).
Radiation was detected on wreckage of a Russian missile used to arm a Geran‑2 drone in Chernihiv, suggesting possible radiological payload experimentation (Source: @UkraineNow/81359).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are intensifying combined arms pressure across multiple fronts, employing artillery, tactical aviation and expanding UAV corridors, particularly in the north and south. Ukraine's successful strike on the Snizhne UAV training centre degrades Russian drone capabilities but does not halt the influx of new UAV groups. The announced potential northern offensive from Belarus raises the risk of a new front opening, prompting extensive SBU counter‑intelligence actions.
sections.regional_response: Western allies continue to supply advanced UAVs with AI‑enabled targeting, enhancing Ukrainian air‑defence and strike capacity. NATO members are monitoring the possible Belarusian staging area, while Russia's nuclear‑related exercises indicate a broader strategic posturing. Ukraine's security services have tightened checks in the northern oblasts to mitigate infiltration risks.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths shows a pattern of north‑east and south‑east corridors that could be exploited for future strikes; SIGINT should focus on command‑and‑control nodes in Snizhne and Sumy. The radiation finding on Geran‑2 missile components warrants further analysis for potential WMD implications. Continued assessment of Belarusian troop movements is critical given Zelensky's warning of a Chernigov‑Kiev axis offensive.
